input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition that keys off the project name convention (e.g. `*.Tests`). In your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Shared properties for all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- MTP settings — only for test projects -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** `$(MSBuildProjectName)` is the project filename without the extension, available before any NuGet packages are resolved, so the condition is evaluated early enough.

**Alternative** — if your test projects don't follow a naming convention, you can instead set a sentinel property inside each test `.csproj`:

```xml
<!-- In each test csproj -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Then in a **`Directory.Build.targets`** (not `.props`, so it runs *after* project files are evaluated):

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

The naming-convention approach is simpler; the sentinel approach is more explicit. Pick whichever fits your repo.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditionals. I can answer directly.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks whether the project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (or uses a naming convention). The most robust way is to condition on the `IsTestProject` property that the .NET Test SDK sets, or to use a naming convention like `*.Tests.csproj`.


I'll provide a straightforward, practical solution for this.
